system: point-displacement string modal observer with a declared reference-shape ceiling

state_variables.name: q1
state_variables.meaning: amplitude of first fixed-end sine mode

state_variables.name: q2
state_variables.meaning: amplitude of second fixed-end sine mode

state_variables.name: q3
state_variables.meaning: amplitude of third fixed-end sine mode

ideal: point displacement y at x0 = L_string / 4 as a linear functional of q1, q2, q3
practical: calibrated point displacement pickup plus a declared reference actuation or clamping protocol for the modal shape r

control_variables.name: reference_shape
control_variables.meaning: declared modal right inverse r satisfying C r = 1 for unit point displacement

regime_assumptions: same small-amplitude fixed-end three-mode truncation as string_fixed_end_three_mode
point sensor is calibrated to a linear displacement measurement at x0 = L_string / 4
observer row is C_n = sqrt(2 / L_string) sin(n pi x0 / L_string) for n = 1, 2, 3
reference shape is declared as r = C^T / (C C^T), the minimum Euclidean modal-amplitude right inverse for unit y
ceiling T is the stiffness of the declared reference shape, T = r^T K r
relaxed visible precision Phi is the minimum-energy stiffness over all q satisfying C q = y
modes above n = 3, damping, drive, noise, and point-spread effects are outside this card
hidden-load interpretation is relative to the declared reference ceiling, not intrinsic to the point sensor alone
equation_hook: y = C q; r = C^T / (C C^T); T = r^T K r; Phi = (C K^{-1} C^T)^{-1}

units_and_dimensions.q_n: m sqrt(m) under the declared orthonormal spatial basis convention
units_and_dimensions.T: reference-shape stiffness for unit point displacement
units_and_dimensions.Phi_y: relaxed quotient stiffness for the declared point displacement channel
units_and_dimensions.T_minus_Phi: stiffness gap attributable to relaxation away from the declared reference shape

coordinate_note: This card is not a visible-block ceiling in the original modal coordinates. The ceiling is a declared reference-shape stiffness for the unit point displacement channel; hidden-load readout is therefore a reference-relaxation statement, not an intrinsic property of C alone.

module_object.kind: observed finite modal stiffness quotient with declared reference ceiling
module_object.description: three-mode stiffness Hessian K observed through one point-displacement row C, plus reference-shape ceiling T = r^T K r

derivation_status: exact for the declared three-mode fixed-end truncation, point sensor position, and reference-shape right inverse

observer_map.description: observe y at x0 = L_string / 4 through the first three sine modes

ceiling_or_reference.description: T = r^T K r with r = C^T / (C C^T), a declared unit-output reference shape rather than a canonical visible block

positive_scope: This card shows that a point-sensor modal observer can support a hidden-load readout only after a reference shape and ceiling are declared.
boundary: It does not claim that the point sensor alone defines a canonical ceiling, infer full modal amplitudes, attach modes above n = 3, model noise, or attach damping and drive response.
failure_mode: The hidden-load attachment fails if the reference shape is not physically implemented or declared, if the ceiling does not dominate the relaxed visible precision, or if the reference-based gap is treated as an intrinsic sensor invariant.
next_measurement: Implement two point-displacement protocols at x0 = L_string / 4: one constrained to the declared reference shape and one relaxed over compatible modal amplitudes, then compare the measured stiffness gap with T - Phi.
